---
name: nezam-ia-taxonomy
description: Define navigation hierarchy, URL structure, breadcrumb logic, and taxonomy maps before content creation.
version: 1.0.0

---
# Purpose

Lock site information architecture before content or visuals: navigation labels, URL hierarchy, breadcrumbs, and a controlled taxonomy of categories/tags. Single-responsibility: IA + URL contract.

# Inputs

- Keyword and intent map from `docs/specs/SEO_RESEARCH.md`.
- Topical clusters from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-topical-authority/SKILL.md`.
- Audience personas and primary tasks from `docs/specs/prd/PRD.md`.
- Existing nav (if redesign) and analytics drop-off data.

# Step-by-Step Workflow

1. Run a tree test or card sort with target users (or proxy) before locking labels.
2. Author `docs/specs/IA_CONTENT.md`: top-level nav, secondary nav, footer, utility nav.
3. Define URL hierarchy rules: lowercase, hyphenated, ≤ 3 segments, stable, lowercase locale prefix.
4. Specify breadcrumb logic and `BreadcrumbList` schema alignment.
5. Build a controlled taxonomy: category (1:1), tags (n:m), facets; document allowed values.
6. Define redirect map for legacy URLs (301 with rationale).
7. Validate accessibility of nav: semantic landmarks, keyboard support, skip-links.

# Validation & Metrics

- Tree test top-task success rate ≥ 80% (or documented justification).
- URL hierarchy follows the contract; no exceptions without recorded decision.
- Every page has breadcrumbs and `BreadcrumbList` JSON-LD candidate.
- Nav passes axe-core landmark and keyboard checks.

# Output Format

- `docs/specs/IA_CONTENT.md` (nav map, URL hierarchy, breadcrumb spec).
- Taxonomy schema (categories, tags, facets, allowed values).
- Redirect map (`from → to`, status, reason).
- Card-sort/tree-test report.

# Integration Hooks

- `/PLAN ia` produces the artifact.
- `/PLAN content` and `/PLAN design` depend on locked IA.
- `/SCAN a11y` validates nav landmarks.
- Pairs with `@.cursor/skills/nezam-topical-authority/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-content-modeling/SKILL.md`.
- Honors `[.cursor/rules/sdd-design.mdc](.cursor/rules/sdd-design.mdc)` and `[.cursor/rules/workspace-orchestration.mdc](.cursor/rules/workspace-orchestration.mdc)`.

# Anti-Patterns

- Locking visuals before IA is approved.
- "Mega menus" with no information scent.
- URL changes during redesign without 301 redirects.
- Tag explosion (every author invents tags).
- Hidden nav patterns that fail keyboard or screen-reader users.

# External Reference

- Rosenfeld, Morville, Arango — Information Architecture (4th ed.).
- W3C ARIA Authoring Practices: navigation patterns (current).
- Schema.org `BreadcrumbList` (https://schema.org/BreadcrumbList).
- Closest skills.sh/official analog: information-architecture / sitemap-builder.
